'''Ian Pickavance''' (born 20 September 1968) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|pr}}. He started his career at [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]], and went on play for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Pickavance was born in [[St Helens, Merseyside|St. Helens]], [[Lancashire]], [[England]].

==Career==
===Club career===
After a successful trial, Pickavance was signed by [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] in February 1989.<ref>{{cite news |title=Star trialist signs |url=https://www.britishnewspaperarchive.co.uk/viewer/bl/0003938/19890216/052/0052 |work=Salford Advertiser |date=16 February 1989 |page=52 |via=[[British Newspaper Archive]] |url-access=subscription}}</ref> He joined [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in 1993.<ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby League: Team-by-team guide to the Stones Bitter Championship |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-teambyteam-guide-to-the-stones-bitter-championship-1463950.html |website=The Independent |access-date=16 February 2024 |date=28 August 1993}}</ref>

Pickavance played at {{rlp|pr}} (replaced by [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] [[Vila Matautia]] on 25-minutes) in St Helens' 16–25 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1995–96 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1995–96 Rugby Football League season|1995–96 season]] at [[John Smith's Stadium|Alfred McAlpine Stadium]], [[Huddersfield]] on Saturday 13 January 1996.<ref name="13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|title=13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015|archive-date=7 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181007005311/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Pickavance played for St Helens from the interchange bench in the [[1996 Challenge Cup]] Final, scoring a try in the second half and helping his team to a 40–32 victory over [[Bradford Bulls]].<ref>{{cite web|title=steveprescottfoundation.co.uk|url=http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|work=Steve Prescott Stats|publisher=Steve Prescott Foundation|accessdate=3 June 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120808052156/http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|archive-date=8 August 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===International===
Pickavance made one appearance at {{rlp|sr}} for Ireland in the 22–24 defeat to [[France national rugby league team|France]] on 4 Nov 1998 in the 1998 European Tri-nations Championship.

==Personal life==
Ian Pickavance's father, Joe Pickavance, played rugby league for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the 1960s. His grandfather, also named Joe, served as chairman at St Helens.<ref>{{cite web |title=Joe laid foundation of Saints' success |url=https://www.lancashiretelegraph.co.uk/news/6022447.joe-laid-foundation-saints-success/ |website=Lancashire Telegraph |access-date=16 February 2024 |date=28 June 2001}}</ref>
